Ad Text:
Reporting to the Head of Sales Force Effectiveness, this role plays a critical part in improving the efficiency and effectiveness of our sales organisation through robust analytics, insightful reporting and strong sales operations discipline.

This is a fantastic opportunity for someone who enjoys turning data into actionable insights, partnering closely with Sales leaders, and driving continuous improvement across sales performance, reporting and incentives.

About the role

In this role, you will be responsible for strengthening sales operations and analytics capability across the business. You will ensure the sales team has timely, accurate and meaningful insights to support fact-based decision making and profitable growth.

Key responsibilities include:


Driving improvements in sales efficiency and effectiveness through best practice sales methodology and operational rigour

Delivering insightful analysis of merchant, customer and segment performance, identifying opportunities for improvement

Developing, owning and enhancing sales reports, dashboards and performance analytics across all segments

Reviewing and streamlining existing reporting to ensure relevance, efficiency and impact

Establishing and managing a consistent sales reporting cadence, including target and quota reviews

Playing a key role in the management and ongoing improvement of the ANZ Sales Incentive Program

Providing clear performance insights aligned to the sales incentive plan and supporting course-correction where required

Supporting pricing analytics to enable effective pricing mechanisms and profitable volume growth

Leading and developing direct reports, fostering a high-performance and insight-led culture



About you

You are an analytical and commercially savvy sales operations professional who thrives on creating clarity from complexity. You're comfortable working with senior stakeholders and are confident influencing outcomes through data-led insights.

You will bring:


Strong experience in sales operations, analytics or sales performance roles

A proven ability to translate data into practical, actionable insights for sales teams and leaders

Solid experience using PowerBi
Experience building and managing sales dashboards, reports and performance frameworks

A solid understanding of sales incentives, targets and performance management

Strong stakeholder engagement skills and the ability to work cross-functionally

People leadership experience, with a focus on capability development and performance


Why join us?

You will be joining a collaborative and high-performing sales environment where insights genuinely shape decision making.

This role offers visibility, impact and the opportunity to influence how sales performance is measured and optimised across the business.


If you are passionate about sales analytics, enjoy partnering with Sales leaders, and want to make a tangible impact, we would love to hear from you.

As our highly valued Portfolio Systems & Reporting Manager based in Brisbane, your role will lead how we design, optimise and use portfolio systems and reporting across the enterprise, supporting our purpose of helping every Australian own their own home.

You'll make an impact by:

Leading the design and continuous improvement of portfolio systems and reporting frameworks that support governance and decision-making
Ensuring portfolio data is accurate, consistent and trusted, enabling confident decision-making
Building clear, executive-level dashboards and insights that drive prioritisation and performance
Reducing manual effort and delivering faster, more meaningful insights
Enabling delivery teams with the right tools, frameworks and guidance to set initiatives up for success
Driving adoption of best-practice tools (JIRA, Confluence, SharePoint, Teams) across the portfolio
Providing analysis and insights on portfolio performance, risks and dependencies to support strategic planning and prioritisation
Influencing senior stakeholders and aligning teams to improve delivery outcomes across the enterprise

Do things differently with us
This isn't a reporting role that just maintains the status quo. You'll shape how portfolio delivery works across the bank.

You'll sit at the centre of Enterprise Delivery, working across portfolios, capabilities and change functions to lift performance and consistency. You'll challenge existing ways of working, simplify complexity and embed systems and practices that actually get used.

If you're someone who sees disconnected tools, inconsistent reporting and manual processes as an opportunity to improve how things work, this is where you can make a real impact.

To succeed in this role, you will have:

Demonstrated experience managing portfolio or enterprise systems, including implementation, optimisation and integration of tools such as PPM or reporting platforms
Strong experience with Atlassian and Microsoft tooling (JIRA, Confluence, SharePoint, Teams)
Proven ability to develop executive-level dashboards, performance metrics and reporting outputs
Experience improving data quality, governance and reporting standards across complex environments
Ability to partner with senior stakeholders and translate business needs into practical system and reporting solutions
A track record of driving process improvements to streamline activities and reduce manual effort
Relevant qualifications in project/program management, business, IT or a related discipline
Certifications in Agile, PRINCE2, MSP, P3O or similar is desirable

Why Great Southern Bank?
Whatever your role with us, you'll be part of a force for good. We're owned by our customers right here in Australia, so our success benefits everyday people and families. Working at Great Southern Bank, you'll also enjoy a range of fantastic benefits, some examples include:

Helping you unlock a brighter future by investing in your development and wellbeing, offering you study leave, a day for you and professional wellbeing coaching.
Benefits that support you and your wellbeing so that you can thrive at work and at home, today and tomorrow. Enjoy access to our wellbeing centre and the flexibility to work from home two days a week.
Helping you to achieve your financial goals and positively impact your family and community. We offer discounts on a wide range of Great Southern Bank products and services such as savings and transaction accounts, insurance, home loans and more!

Imagine working for a bank that truly helps people. You can.

Questions? Please contact our Talent Acquisition team at car••••@gsb.com.au

At Great Southern Bank, we're building a workplace where everyone belongs. We value diverse backgrounds, cultures, abilities and experiences - including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, veterans, people with disability, and the LGBTQ+ community - because it makes us stronger for our people, customers and communities. Through flexible working, collaboration and fair, inclusive decision-making, we empower our people to do their best every day.

We move with purpose, are curious about what's next, remain focused on what matters most, and are always ready to find smarter ways to help our customers and communities thrive. Our people bring energy and initiative to a fast-changing environment, making thoughtful decisions that balance doing good for our customers with driving sustainable success for our business. If you enjoy working in a place where priorities evolve, ideas are valued, and every action contributes to something bigger, you'll feel right at home here at Great Southern Bank.

analyse established business processes and procedures to identify improvements in line with the deliverables of the Phase 4 Enhancement Project;

About the Role

Compact Lifts is seeking an experienced Management Consultant to analyse, improve and optimise our organisational systems, sales processes, and commercial operations. This is a full-time, Melbourne-based position.

Working across our business units, you will assess current operational structures, identify inefficiencies, and develop and implement solutions to improve business performance. Your work will span sales operations, process design, CRM systems, workflow automation, and performance reporting.

Key Responsibilities

·         Assess and evaluate existing organisational structures, business processes, systems, and workflows across sales, marketing, and operational functions to identify inefficiencies and improvement opportunities.

·         Consult with business leaders, sales managers, and operational staff to understand current process shortcomings, system limitations, and commercial performance gaps.

·         Develop strategies, frameworks, and implementation plans to improve organisational efficiency, sales conversion performance, and commercial outcomes.

·         Analyse and document current workflow processes, data flows, and system configurations, producing structured records including process maps, SOPs, user guides, and system documentation.

·         Design and recommend solutions to resolve organisational and operational problems, including CRM configuration, pipeline architecture, lifecycle stage design, and workflow automation.

·         Oversee the implementation of approved recommendations, including issuing revised procedures, configuration changes, and supporting documentation across teams.

·         Review operational procedures and reporting outputs, advising leadership on departures from agreed standards and recommending corrective actions.

·         Build and maintain real-time performance reporting frameworks, funnel metrics, and management dashboards to support data-driven decision-making.

·         Deliver structured change management activities, including staff training, adoption monitoring, and ongoing system governance to embed process improvements.

·         Evaluate emerging technologies and system capabilities for commercial relevance and present assessed recommendations to leadership with supporting business cases.

·         Scale and adapt approved organisational frameworks across multiple business units, ensuring consistency while accommodating operational differences.

·         Conduct organisational studies and reviews to improve management methods and structures.

·         Prepare recommendations for senior management regarding changes to business processes and organisational procedures.

·         Advise management on policies and strategies to improve efficiency and profitability.




Skills and Experience Required

Experience

·         Proven experience analysing and redesigning business processes, operational workflows, and organisational systems in a commercial environment.

·         Demonstrated experience developing and implementing solutions to improve sales performance, process efficiency, and forecast accuracy.

·         Experience consulting with and advising senior stakeholders, including executives, sales leadership, and operational teams.

·         Track record of delivering change management, system adoption, and staff training initiatives.

·         Experience producing detailed process documentation, workflow analysis, SOPs, and governance frameworks.

·         Experience working across multi-brand or multi-unit business structures (preferred).




Skills and Capabilities

·         Strong analytical and problem-solving capability, with the ability to evaluate complex systems and develop practical, commercially-grounded solutions.

·         Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills across executive, operational, and frontline levels.

·         Ability to translate business requirements into system configurations, process designs, and governance frameworks.

·         Strong reporting and data analysis skills, including performance dashboards, funnel metrics, and management reporting.

·         Structured approach to documentation, process design, and change management.

·         Self-directed, with the ability to lead projects and drive outcomes independently.




Qualifications

·         Bachelor's degree or higher in Business, Commerce, Management, Information Systems, or a related discipline

·         Candidates with at least five years of relevant experience in lieu of formal qualifications will be considered.

·         Relevant professional certifications or postgraduate qualifications are highly regarded.

Role Overview

Position: Senior Consultant - Transformation

Location: Melbourne (Hybrid: 3 days on-site)

Initial Duration: 1st week of June - 31st July, with a view to move to longer term contract for the delivery phase

The Opportunity

We are seeking a heavyweight, highly autonomous Senior Consultant - Transformation to spearhead the critical review and bottom-up re-set of a large-scale enterprise transformation program.

This is a two-phased engagement. Initially, you will operate in a strategic advisory capacity, conducting a rapid diagnostic of the current state, defining priorities, and designing the future roadmap. For the right candidate, this will transition into a long-term execution role, where you will own and deliver the very initiatives you have shaped.

Key Responsibilities

In this role, you will be entirely accountable for shaping the design and structure of the program. Your initial focus will center on:

Program Diagnostic & Review: Assess the current state of the large-scale transformation program. Identify bottlenecks, evaluate where the program is lagging, and determine immediate priorities.

Strategic Re-set & Design: Re-architect the program from the bottom-up. Establish robust frameworks, optimize mobilization strategies, and redefine ways of working and technology alignment.

Stakeholder Engagement & Elicitation: Partner closely with executive-level stakeholders to elicit complex business requirements, manage expectations, and align on desired outcomes.

Roadmapping & Prioritization: Restructure the program portfolio. Facilitate rigorous prioritization frameworks to determine what the new initiatives need to be, when they should launch, and what solutions must be delivered.

Recommendations: Present clear, data-driven recommendations and a finalized strategic roadmap to the executive steering committee.

About You

We need a "shaper" who can operate with a high degree of ambiguity and take total ownership.

Seniority & Autonomy: Proven track record operating at a Senior Consultant or Advisory level, ideally from a Consulting background. You are comfortable working completely independently.

Transformation Expertise: Deep experience reviewing, rescuing, or resetting troubled, large-scale enterprise transformation programs (incorporating people, process, and technology).

Executive Presence: Exceptional stakeholder management skills with a demonstrated ability to influence, challenge, and advise C-suite and executive leaders.

Strategic Architecture: Strong capability in designing program frameworks, operating models, and roadmaps from scratch.

Delivery Acumen: While the start is strategic, you possess the delivery grit required to transition into the execution phase later this year.

Domain Knowledge: Experience working in the Consulting and Higher Education space is required to successfully deliver this role
Why Apply?

High Impact: You will have the mandate to completely reshape a major program from the ground up.

Longevity: A seamless bridge from a short-term advisory piece into a multi-month/year delivery pipeline.

We are currently seeking a Senior Data Engineer who is passionate about driving modern data solutions within an industry leading Australian Employer of Choice. This role is permanent, full time.

Joining NTI’s Insights & Analytics team, you’ll play a strategic role in shaping and delivering scalable, secure, and high-performing data infrastructure that underpins NTI’s analytics, actuarial and data science platforms. You’ll lead the design of end-to-end data solutions, influence data platform strategy, and work cross-functionally to deliver trusted, high-quality data assets that enable data-driven decision-making across the business.

Working alongside experienced engineers, architects and analytics professionals, you’ll bring deep technical expertise while mentoring others and championing best practice across data engineering, cloud platforms and data governance — contributing to high-impact initiatives that drive innovation and operational efficiency.

About the role

Day to day, you will:

Lead the design, development and optimisation of scalable data pipelines and ETL/ELT processes across cloud and on-premises platforms

Design and implement robust data architectures that support analytics, reporting and data science use cases

Drive the adoption of modern data engineering practices, including DevOps, CI/CD and automation of data workflows

Develop and maintain high-quality, curated data assets for use across multiple business domains

Optimise data ingestion, transformation and delivery processes to ensure performance, reliability and scalability

Establish and enforce data governance, security and quality standards across the data lifecycle

Collaborate with stakeholders across IT, Insights & Analytics and the wider business to translate complex requirements into technical solutions

Provide technical leadership, mentorship and guidance to data engineers and broader analytics teams

Evaluate and implement emerging tools, technologies and frameworks to continuously improve NTI’s data ecosystem

Create and maintain comprehensive technical documentation for data solutions, architectures and processes

What we would like to see from you

From a personal perspective, we’d love to see:

A strategic mindset with the ability to balance big-picture thinking and hands-on delivery

Strong communication skills, with confidence engaging stakeholders across technical and non-technical audiences

A proactive approach to problem solving, with the ability to navigate complexity and ambiguity

A collaborative mindset and passion for mentoring and uplifting team capability

A continuous improvement focus, with curiosity to explore emerging technologies and practices

From a technical perspective, we’d love to see the following in your application:

Tertiary qualification in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Technology or a related discipline

Extensive experience (7+ years) in data engineering, including enterprise data platforms and cloud environments

Strong expertise in SQL and data modelling, with proven experience in data warehouse design and large-scale data solutions

Hands-on experience with ETL/ELT tools and frameworks (e.g. SSIS, dbt, Airflow, Azure Data Factory or similar)

Proficiency in programming languages such as Python (or similar) for data processing and automation

Demonstrated experience working with cloud data platforms (e.g. Azure, AWS or GCP)

Experience designing and integrating APIs and working with streaming or real-time data solutions (e.g. Kafka or similar)

Strong understanding of data governance, data quality and information security practices

Experience implementing modern data engineering principles, including CI/CD and DevOps practices

Ability to design scalable data architectures and translate business requirements into robust technical solutions

What to expect when you're a part of the NTI family:

Boasting close to 50 years' experience and backed by two of Australia's largest insurers (CGU and Vero), you'll enjoy stability, a great brand reputation and a team of experts to learn from. We are committed to being a Best Employer of the Best People and as such, we offer:

A real and tangible commitment to your development and ongoing study

Competitive annual incentive program of up to 15% of your salary package

Up to 50% discount on a large range of personal insurance products

Access to discounts on over 350 retailers across Australia

The ability to apply for flexible working arrangements (subject to relevant NTI policies) + more!

At NTI, we're committed to providing an environment where our team members feel comfortable to be themselves and valued for their unique perspectives and contributions.

Ready to apply? We can't wait to read your application!

We'd love for you to apply with a resume and cover letter explaining why you'd like to join our team.

Our application process is tailored to each role based on the unique requirements. You may be required to complete phone and face to face interviews, and qualification and reference checks as part of the application process.

Job Title: Senior Manager Advanced Analytics - Data Science and AI
Ad Text:
Your Impact

 

You will define and execute an enterprise-wide strategy spanning advanced analytics, data science, AI and GenAI, ensuring alignment with business priorities, technology direction and long-term value creation. Working in close partnership with senior and executive leaders, you will translate complex analytical and AI concepts into clear, actionable insights that drive stronger decision-making and commercial outcomes. 

 

Key Responsibilities 

 

Define and deliver Asahi’s enterprise strategy and multi-year roadmap for analytics, data science, AI and GenAI 
Lead the AI Centre of Excellence, setting standards and scalable delivery models 
Oversee delivery of high-impact analytics, AI/ML and GenAI solutions 
Partner with senior leaders to shape and prioritise value-led use cases 
Establish and embed governance, responsible AI, risk and compliance frameworks 
Build, lead and develop high-performing multidisciplinary teams and partners 
Uplift enterprise capability through enablement, tooling and operating-model integration 
Manage strategic technology and delivery partners to maximise outcomes

Extensive experience in data science, AI/ML or advanced analytics leadership roles

Headquartered in Melbourne and with facilities right across Australia and New Zealand, you’ll find Asahi is a place where talent is nurtured, and ambition is rewarded. If you’re ready to toast your future, we’re ready to meet you.

About this role
As the Senior Detection & Response Analyst, you will protect nib by delivering high-quality threat intelligence and detection & response capability. You will achieve this by monitoring and analysing the evolving threat landscape, producing timely stakeholder reporting, and translating intelligence into improved detections, investigations, and threat-informed security recommendations.

Day to day you will:


Triage alerts and investigate security incidents
Build detections and apply threat intelligence
Automate workflows and optimise response processes
Lead incident response, containment and remediation do
Produce reporting, documentation and insights
Identify vulnerabilities and strengthen security practices
Mentor analysts and support team delivery

About you
You bring strong foundations in incident response, with hands-on experience across containment, investigation and remediation. Comfortable working in fast-paced environments, you understand how to manage security incidents end-to-end and contribute to effective, timely outcomes.

You have practical experience with core security tools such as SIEM, EDR and network detection technologies. You're confident navigating security platforms, analysing alerts and leveraging tooling to support investigations and strengthen detection capability.

Backed by solid knowledge of public cloud, network and endpoint fundamentals, you understand how systems communicate and how they can be exploited. With a working knowledge of TCP/IP, Windows and Linux environments, you can identify common attack vectors and apply this insight to real-world security scenarios.

You'll bring:

Ability to analyse security alerts, connect related events, and determine root causes
Can clearly articulate technical findings in reports or during investigations, both written and verbally
Understanding of data protection principles and common security compliance frameworks
Entry- to mid-level security certification (CompTIA Security+, GIAC GSEC, or similar) (desired)
Basic ability to automate repetitive tasks in at least one language (Python, PowerShell, etc.) (desired)
Knowledge of cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP) and related security concepts (desired)
Experience with log aggregation and analysis, extracting insights from large datasets (desired)
Experience in scanning tools and interpreting vulnerability reports (desired)
Specific experience configuring and managing cloud security controls (DLP, CASB, etc.) (desired)

We know some people only apply when they meet every requirement. We're always on the lookout for curious individuals who will add to the culture - so if this role resonates with you and you have relevant experience, we'd love to hear from you!

Introduction

As a member the Promontory Australia team at IBM you will contribute to the firm's efforts in working with clients on challenging strategic, risk management, and regulatory compliance issues.

Our team of specialists offers the following services across Prudential, Conduct and AML Regulation:

· Risk Management – Providing advisory and assurance services regarding organisational transformation across risk management, governance, culture, accountability and compliance.

· Remediation Services – Assurance to regulators and firms over customer focused remediation programs.

· Regulatory Framework – Advising regulators and industry associations on building regulatory frameworks.

· Regulatory Liaison – Helping our clients navigate relationships with regulators.

· RegTech – Working with IBM teams and leveraging their technology assets in advising clients on AI based solutions and associated risk governance.

We have a diverse client base in Australia, which includes some of Australia's largest financial institutions and clients drawn from the public sector.

As a Principal, you will be responsible for managing risk, regulatory and compliance engagements on a day-to-day basis, analysing complex problems, developing recommendations to address client issues and heavily involved in reports and other deliverables. A strong consulting background with experience in non-financial risk management (including operational risk and resilience) and or superannuation is preferable. You will also be expected to draw upon your insights and networks to identify business opportunities for IBM Promontory to pursue.




Roles and Responsibilities

To be successful in this role a candidate must possess strong leadership skills and have the stature to engage effectively with clients and potential clients at all levels. The candidate will also need to inspire and work effectively with team members drawn from a range of technical disciplines. Interact comfortably with executive management across industry and regulatory authorities. Key responsibilities will include:

Manage projects and act as client point of contact on a day-to-day basis contributing to engagements being delivered to budget and meeting profit and realisation targets.

Undertake analysis as directed and take responsibility for the preparation of project deliverables, including reports and presentations.

Interact comfortably with executive management of and regulatory authorities, generally with a Managing Director present.

Strong analytical skills, oral skills, with demonstrated ability to communicate clearly, simply and logically.

Strong written communication skills with, the ability to construct well-founded, clear, and concise analyses and recommendations.

Ability to handle complex information, solve problems, and manage multiple tasks.

Ability to work closely with, and communicate effectively with, clients and within all levels of the organisation.

Strong commitment to integrity and ethics.

The candidate will also be required to build and retain relationships across the industry and to identify opportunities for further work. This includes the ability to establish and maintain positive relationships and build teams that are motivated to succeed.




Required Professional and Technical Expertise

· An undergraduate degree in either Accounting, Commerce, Finance, or Law - with postgraduate qualifications highly regarded.

· At least 6 years experience in Financial services, Consulting, Regulator, Compliance, Risk management, Governance or legal.

· Track record working in a fast-paced and unstructured environment with aptitude for identifying and developing innovative solutions to client challenges.

· Analytics and data: Proven track record of successfully delivering highly analytical and data intensive work. Strong understanding of data governance and experience in working with large and unstructured datasets to draw meaningful and actionable insights and outcomes.

· AI: A strong understanding of AI fundamentals, including AI governance, and model development and application.

· Expert level capabilities in Excel (incl. advanced excel functionality and VBA / Macro development). Expert level capabilities in working with large datasets in SQL.




Preferred Professional and Technical Expertise

· Tertiary qualifications in Commerce, Finance, Law or Business Administration with postgraduate qualifications highly regarded.

· Specialising in data analytics, risk management, assurance, and regulatory compliance.

· Experience developing, testing and validating the end-to-end modelling process and knowledge of a variety of tools including Alteryx, R and Python.




About the Business Unit:

IBM Promontory, a business unit of IBM Consulting, is the premier consulting firm for financial services companies. IBM Promontory excels at helping clients resolve critical issues, particularly those with a regulatory dimension. Promontory professionals have unparalleled regulatory experience and insight, and provide our clients with frank, proactive advice informed by best practices and regulatory expectations.

Promontory Australia is a boutique operation and maintains links to the US and UK. Our consultants in Australia have diverse backgrounds from regulatory authorities, industry and consulting.

In recent years Promontory has completed a number of high profile engagements with Australian financial institutions and due to a strong pipeline of work we are looking to expand our team with a highly skilled, adaptable and dependable individual to join the high performing Sydney office.

The University of Melbourne

Position Description: JR-012187_Director of Statistical Consulting Centre PD.pdf

Introduction

A career in IBM Consulting is built on long-term client relationships and close collaboration worldwide. You’ll work with leading companies across industries, helping them shape their hybrid cloud and AI journeys. With support from our strategic partners, robust IBM technology, and Red Hat, you’ll have the tools to drive meaningful change and accelerate client impact. At IBM Consulting, curiosity fuels success. You’ll be encouraged to challenge the norm, explore new ideas, and create innovative solutions that deliver real results. Our culture of growth and empathy focuses on your long-term career development while valuing your unique skills and experiences.

Your role and responsibilities

As a Package Consultant at IBM, you will design, develop, and re-engineer complex application components and integrate software packages to drive mission-critical client results. You will apply a blend of consultative skills and technical expertise to facilitate the hybrid cloud and AI journey for global organizations.

A SAP Data Migration Consultant plays a vital role in ensuring data integrity and consistency during the implementation of SAP projects by managing the end-to-end migration lifecycle.

Required education

Bachelor's Degree

Required technical and professional expertise

1. Strategy & Project Planning

Develop and execute comprehensive data migration strategies for SAP Hybrid (Selective Data Transition, Bluefield, Rapid Move) or traditional Brownfield/Greenfield projects.

Define scope, objectives, and deliverables while identifying and mitigating risks to ensure projects stay within timeline and budget.

Implement "early warning systems" to proactively address potential delays in the migration timeline.

2. Data Assessment & Quality Management

Assess the readiness and quality of legacy data, defining robust rules for cleansing, enrichment, and validation.

Oversee data mapping and transformation logic specifically for EAM data structures.

Implement data modeling and quality management techniques to ensure accurate data reflects in the target system.

3. Technical Execution & ETL

Design, develop, and execute migration programs and scripts using tools like SNP CrystalBridge or SAP standard migration tools.

Coordinate the end-to-end Extraction, Transformation, and Loading (ETL) process from legacy systems to SAP.

Troubleshoot technical migration activities promptly to resolve data-related issues.

4. Testing & Validation

Design and execute robust test plans, including unit, integration, and User Acceptance Tests (UAT), to guarantee functionality after migration.

Validate migrated data against business and regulatory requirements to ensure absolute data integrity.

5. Stakeholder Collaboration & Mentorship

Partner with business stakeholders to understand data requirements and validation criteria.

Collaborate with global experts, including the off shore centre and regional project teams, to maintain alignment on delivery.

Provide guidance and mentorship to team members and analysts to foster an efficient working environment.

6. Documentation, Training & Compliance

Maintain comprehensive documentation of migration processes, mapping rules, and validation criteria.

Provide training and support to business users on migration tools and offer post-migration support.

Ensure all activities comply with data security measures and organizational policies to protect sensitive information.

7. Continous Improvement:

Identify improvement opportunities for improving data migration processes and methodologies

Stay updated with the latest SAP data migration tools and technologies

Required Professional and Technical Expertise

Experience: Minimum 8 years of experience in SAP Data Migration projects (Greenfield, Brownfield, or Hybrid/Selective Data Transition).

Functional Expertise: Strong understanding of SAP data structures and business processes.

Platform Knowledge: Proven expertise in SAP S/4HANA migration using SNP tools (such as CrystalBridge) or standard SAP tools.

Soft Skills: Exceptional analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills with the ability to work in fast-paced, agile environments.

Location: Sydney or Melbourne




ABOUT BUSINESS UNIT

IBM Consulting is IBM’s consulting and global professional services business, with market leading capabilities in business and technology transformation. With deep expertise in many industries, we offer strategy, experience, technology, and operations services to many of the most innovative and valuable companies in the world. Our people are focused on accelerating our clients’ businesses through the power of collaboration. We believe in the power of technology responsibly used to help people, partners and the planet.

YOUR LIFE @ IBM

In a world where technology never stands still, we understand that, dedication to our clients success, innovation that matters, and trust and personal responsibility in all our relationships, lives in what we do as IBMers as we strive to be the catalyst that makes the world work better.

Being an IBMer means you’ll be able to learn and develop yourself and your career, you’ll be encouraged to be courageous and experiment everyday, all whilst having continuous trust and support in an environment where everyone can thrive whatever their personal or professional background.

Our IBMers are growth minded, always staying curious, open to feedback and learning new information and skills to constantly transform themselves and our company. They are trusted to provide on-going feedback to help other IBMers grow, as well as collaborate with colleagues keeping in mind a team focused approach to include different perspectives to drive exceptional outcomes for our customers. The courage our IBMers have to make critical decisions everyday is essential to IBM becoming the catalyst for progress, always embracing challenges with resources they have to hand, a can-do attitude and always striving for an outcome focused approach within everything that they do.

Are you ready to be an IBMer?

ABOUT IBM

IBM’s greatest invention is the IBMer. We believe that through the application of intelligence, reason and science, we can improve business, society and the human condition, bringing the power of an open hybrid cloud and AI strategy to life for our clients and partners around the world.

Restlessly reinventing since 1911, we are not only one of the largest corporate organizations in the world, we’re also one of the biggest technology and consulting employers, with many of the Fortune 500 companies relying on the IBM Cloud to run their business. 

At IBM, we pride ourselves on being an early adopter of artificial intelligence, quantum computing and blockchain. Now it’s time for you to join us on our journey to being a responsible technology innovator and a force for good in the world.

IBM is proud to be an equal-opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, pregnancy, disability, neurodivergence, age, or other characteristics protected by the applicable law. IBM is also committed to compliance with all fair employment practices regarding citizenship and immigration status.

Genus is seeking a Data & Integration Specialist with strong full‑stack development capability to design, build, and support integrations and data pipelines across our enterprise systems.
 
This role sits at the intersection of applications, data, and integration, enabling secure, reliable data flows between business systems and supporting automation, reporting, and operational insights.

You will work closely with IT, data, and business stakeholders to deliver scalable, well‑governed integration solutions that support business outcomes.
 
What you'll be doing

Design and implement system‑to‑system integrations using APIs, webhooks, and event‑driven patterns
Build and maintain data pipelines that support operational reporting and analytics
Develop integration‑enabled applications, services, and utilities using full‑stack development skills
Produce integration artefacts such as solution designs, interface specifications, and context diagrams
Ensure integrations are secure, resilient, monitored, and aligned with enterprise standards
Work with business and IT stakeholders to translate requirements into robust technical solutions
Support production integrations and data pipelines, including troubleshooting and continuous improvement
Contribute to integration and data standards to reduce duplication and improve reuse
Essential experience

Strong experience in data integration and API‑based development
Hands‑on experience building ETL/ELT data pipelines and data transformations
Full‑stack development experience (back‑end and front‑end)
Strong SQL skills and experience working with relational data
Experience working in structured environments (dev/test/prod) with disciplined release practices
Ability to document solutions clearly and communicate with technical and non‑technical stakeholders
Technical skills

Integration technologies: REST APIs, JSON, OAuth2/OIDC, webhooks
Backend development: .NET, Node.js, or Python (one or more)
Front‑end development: modern web frameworks (e.g. React, Angular)
Data engineering concepts: data modelling, validation, quality checks
Version control (Git) and CI/CD concepts
Logging, monitoring, and operational support practices
Desirable

Experience with Microsoft ecosystem and Power Platform integrations
Experience with cloud platforms (e.g. Azure)
Exposure to data governance, security, and audit‑aligned environments
Experience in large or multi‑system enterprise environments
 The Benefits

A stable, ongoing role supporting a growing and diverse business
A collaborative team environment with supportive leadership
Competitive remuneration aligned to your experience
Opportunities to grow your career within a well-established ASX-listed group
GenusPlus Group is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. We strongly encourage applications from women and Aboriginal and First Nations peoples.

Build something better ...


Our client is not looking for someone to simply keep the lights on. They are looking for a commercially minded technology leader who can help scale a growing business through smarter systems, better data, and strong execution. This is a high-impact leadership opportunity within a well-established Australian business with a strong reputation in the power tools and accessories industry. Backed by private equity and growing through acquisition and organic expansion, the business is entering an exciting phase of transformation. With operations across Sydney, Auckland, and Shanghai, the foundations are strong. The focus now is on modernisation, integration, scalability, and continuous improvement.


Reporting directly to the COO and sitting on the Senior Leadership Team, you will play a key role in shaping the future direction of the business.


The Opportunity
This is a hands-on strategic role for someone who enjoys building, improving, and making things happen. You will lead an established team of 5 across ERP, systems integration, data, infrastructure, and operational improvement. The environment is Microsoft-based, with strong operational foundations across warehousing, inventory, supply chain, and B2B eCommerce. You will work closely with operational and commercial leaders to deliver scalable, practical technology outcomes that support business growth.


Technology & Transformation
• Lead technology strategy aligned to business growth
• Drive ERP and systems improvement initiatives
• Improve workflows, automation, and operational efficiency
• Support scalability through smarter technology solutions

Integration & Data
• Lead post-acquisition systems integration and data migration
• Improve reporting, BI, and data capability
• Build a more connected and scalable technology environment

Leadership & Delivery
• Lead and develop the internal technology team
• Manage vendors, consultants, and technology partners
• Deliver cross-functional projects across the business
• Bring structure, accountability, and momentum to delivery

About You

• You are commercially pragmatic, hands-on, and outcomes focused.
• You enjoy solving business problems, simplifying complexity, and building strong relationships across technical and non-technical teams alike.
• You thrive in fast-moving environments, bring people with you through change, and enjoy making a visible impact.

Experience Required

• Technology leadership experience ideally within distribution, manufacturing, logistics, supply chain, or product-based businesses
• Strong ERP, systems integration, and transformation delivery experience
• Exposure to Microsoft environments, data migration, and BI/reporting capability
• Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
• Hands-on leadership style with a strong commercial mindset
• Interest in practical AI and automation that delivers real business value


Benefits
• Lead transformation in a PE-backed growth business
• Senior Leadership Team role reporting directly to the COO
• Real autonomy and influence across the organisation
• Strong culture with modern facilities and employee perks
• 4 days onsite, free lunch Fridays, birthday half-days, ping pong, and Friday drinks

Director, Product Management

Are you an experienced legal industry leader with the commercial acumen to drive the future of Legal Research Solutions across Australia?

We are seeking to appoint an accomplished Director, Product Management to lead our Legal Research Solutions proposition across Australia. Reporting to the Vice President, Legal Content, this senior role will be responsible for driving the commercial strategy, market positioning and overall commercial performance of the Legal Research Solutions portfolio.

About the Role

In this opportunity as Director, Product Management you will:

Drive commercial performance for Legal Research Solutions across Australia, including gross sales, retention, pricing and revenue outcomes.

Develop and execute the Australian GTM strategy for Legal Research Solutions as both a standalone offering and a connected proposition.

Maintain deep market and customer insight by engaging with customers and GTM teams, monitoring legal research needs, competitor activity, industry trends, TR roadmap and future capabilities.

Partner with Product and Editorial teams to influence prioritised roadmap decisions, secure support for key initiatives and ensure product service levels are maintained.

Support sales execution by collaborating with Solutions Consultants, product experts and BDMs on pursuits, deal teams and customer opportunities.

Lead market enablement and launch readiness by working with product marketing teams to deliver current, locally relevant sales resources, collateral and launch plans.

Optimise pricing, partnerships and thought leadership by engaging with strategic pricing leads, enabling commercial partnerships, and representing Legal Research Solutions at deal teams and industry events.

About You

You’re a fit for the role of Director, Product Management if you have the following required qualifications:

10+ years' legal industry experience, supported by relevant legal qualifications and a strong understanding of the legal market, customer needs and industry dynamics.

Extensive direct customer engagement experience, with the credibility to build trusted relationships, understand client priorities and represent the organisation at a senior level.

Strong commercial acumen developed over 10+ years, including the ability to identify growth opportunities, assess commercial impact and drive strategic business outcomes.

Proven senior stakeholder management capability, with experience influencing, aligning and engaging executive-level internal and external stakeholders.

Advanced reporting and financial reporting experience, including interpreting commercial metrics, preparing insights and supporting data-led decision-making.

Demonstrated ability to navigate complex business environments, including ambiguity, competing priorities and cross-functional dependencies.

Experience operating effectively within matrix structures, collaborating across functions, regions and leadership teams to deliver commercial outcomes.

#LI-LK1

What’s in it For You?

Hybrid Work Model: We’ve adopted a flexible hybrid working environment (2-3 days a week in the office depending on the role) for our office-based roles while delivering a seamless experience that is digitally and physically connected.

Flexibility & Work-Life Balance: Flex My Way is a set of supportive workplace policies designed to help manage personal and professional responsibilities, whether caring for family, giving back to the community, or finding time to refresh and reset. This builds upon our flexible work arrangements, including work from anywhere for up to 8 weeks per year, empowering employees to achieve a better work-life balance.

Career Development and Growth: By fostering a culture of continuous learning and skill development, we prepare our talent to tackle tomorrow’s challenges and deliver real-world solutions. Our Grow My Way programming and skills-first approach ensures you have the tools and knowledge to grow, lead, and thrive in an AI-enabled future.

Industry Competitive Benefits: We offer comprehensive benefit plans to include flexible vacation, two company-wide Mental Health Days off, access to the Headspace app, retirement savings, tuition reimbursement, employee incentive programs, and resources for mental, physical, and financial wellbeing.

Culture: Globally recognized, award-winning reputation for inclusion and belonging, flexibility, work-life balance, and more. We live by our values: Obsess over our Customers, Compete to Win, Challenge (Y)our Thinking, Act Fast / Learn Fast, and Stronger Together.

Social Impact: Make an impact in your community with our Social Impact Institute. We offer employees two paid volunteer days off annually and opportunities to get involved with pro-bono consulting projects and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.

Making a Real-World Impact: We are one of the few companies globally that helps its customers pursue justice, truth, and transparency. Together, with the professionals and institutions we serve, we help uphold the rule of law, turn the wheels of commerce, catch bad actors, report the facts, and provide trusted, unbiased information to people all over the world.

About Us

Thomson Reuters informs the way forward by bringing together the trusted content and technology that people and organizations need to make the right decisions. We serve professionals across legal, tax, accounting, compliance, government, and media. Our products combine highly specialized software and insights to empower professionals with the data, intelligence, and solutions needed to make informed decisions, and to help institutions in their pursuit of justice, truth, and transparency. Reuters, part of Thomson Reuters, is a world leading provider of trusted journalism and news.

We are powered by the talents of 26,000 employees across more than 70 countries, where everyone has a chance to contribute and grow professionally in flexible work environments. About the Role

We are seeking an experienced and highly autonomous professional to lead the development and management of business cases for the State Budget 2027/28 process.

This role will oversee the preparation of 7 business cases covering 18 strategic initiatives, working closely with senior stakeholders across government to ensure high-quality, evidence-based submissions aligned with budget priorities and policy objectives.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the end-to-end development of multiple business cases for State Budget 2027/28 initiatives
Manage and coordinate 18 initiatives across several workstreams
Engage with executive stakeholders, policy teams, finance, and operational leads
Develop compelling, evidence-based recommendations and funding proposals
Ensure business cases align with government frameworks, budget requirements, and strategic priorities
Prepare high-quality written documentation, including:
Strategic assessments
Cost-benefit analysis
Financial and economic justifications
Risk assessments
Implementation planning
Drive timelines, dependencies, and approvals with minimal supervision
Provide strategic advice and challenge where required

Essential Requirements

Demonstrated experience developing business cases within a government environment
Strong understanding of state budget processes and government funding submissions
Proven ability to manage multiple complex initiatives simultaneously
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Ability to work autonomously and deliver outcomes in fast-paced environments
Strong analytical, strategic, and written communication capability
Experience coordinating across senior executive and cross-functional teams

Desirable Experience

Experience working on Treasury or central agency submissions
Familiarity with public sector investment frameworks and assurance processes
Background in policy, strategy, transformation, or program delivery

Company Description
Nine is Australia's largest locally owned media company. Working at Nine, you'll have access to a unique range of experiences and opportunities, helping drive the success of the country's most trusted television, radio, digital and publishing brands.
Our content reaches almost every Australian - meaning what we do has real impact. We bring people together, celebrate the big moments, and capture the everyday ones.
Some of our most beloved brands have been part of Australian life for generations, and others - new on the scene - have already found their place firmly in our lives. We're evolving and we need people like you to bring new ideas, innovate and make your mark.


As a Senior Data Engineer at Nine, you will work within the Enterprise Data business unit to build high-performing data products and reduce complexity across our data systems and models. You will partner closely with our Product function to enable innovative solutions, specifically focusing on the Data Publishing space. Your role is critical in processing hundreds of millions of events daily from iconic mastheads including The Australian Financial Review, The Sydney Morning Herald, and The Age. And centralising enterprise data into our data warehouse to inform business operations and strategy.

Day to day you will:

Design, develop, and maintain scalable data pipelines and models that extract, load, and transform data from various sources into the data warehouse.
Lead and guide solution designs, ensuring the team understands the technical "why" and "how" while documenting designs to enhance collective knowledge.
Champion engineering best practices across coding, DevOps, and metadata standardisations to ensure high-quality output within project timelines.
Integrate emerging technologies and AI tools into daily workflows for code suggestions, data profiling, and automated testing.
Collaborate with internal stakeholders and adjacent technology teams, including Cyber Security and Platform Engineering, to maintain organisational standards and resolve incidents.
Work closely with Product Managers to shape data solutions, understand end-user requirements, and support stakeholders through knowledge sharing.

Qualifications

A bachelor's or master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field is highly desirable.
Deep understanding of cloud migration strategies, best practices, and potential pitfalls.

What you'll bring:

Technical Mastery: Fluency in Python and SQL, with a deep understanding of OLTP and OLAP schema design concepts.
Pipeline Expertise: Proven experience leading the design of data pipelines and managing infrastructure using tools like Apache Airflow and DBT.
Cloud & DevOps Skills: Proficiency with major cloud platforms (GCP, AWS, or Azure) and CI/CD solutions like Cloud Build or Concourse.
Modern Engineering Standards: Knowledge of Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform and the ability to leverage AI to support engineering tasks.
Leadership & Communication: Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to lead technical discussions, build consensus, and work effectively with cross-functional teams.
Problem-Solving Mindset: A strong analytical approach and a passion for finding creative solutions to complex technical challenges.


Additional Information

How we work
At Nine, our flexible work options vary by role and team. Depending on the position, this may include flexible hours, hybrid work, or part-time arrangements. We welcome discussing your flexibility needs during the hiring process - just ask the Talent Acquisition team.

Our employee benefits include:

18 weeks paid parental leave with no distinction between primary and secondary carers

Access to 'Employee Exclusives' program - a way of getting closer to our incredible brands, offering unique experiences, behind-the-scenes access, and awesome perks.

Digital newspaper subscription to our mastheads

Annual gift voucher for Stan subscription

More info at Nine Careers

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
We're committed to a safe, respectful and inclusive Nine. From day one, you'll be encouraged to bring your whole self to work and will be supported to perform at your best.

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with disabilities, and of all ages, genders, nationalities, backgrounds and cultures as we recognise the importance and value of diverse perspectives. Should you require any adjustments to the recruitment process, please advise us when you apply.

The Opportunity - M&A Analytics Manager/Senior Manager





Maven Partners are working closely with a leading professional services firm’s Transaction Analytics team is currently seeking dynamic and experienced professionals for Manager and Senior Manager roles within a growing practice. As part of an established and forward-thinking analytics team, you will combine project leadership with hands-on delivery, leveraging data analytics to support clients in making informed decisions during critical transactions.

You will work closely with cross-functional teams on complex engagements, delivering insights that drive value and enhance performance. This role reflects continued investment in the team and offers strong scope for career progression while working on high-impact, transaction-driven engagements.



Key Responsibilities - M&A Analytics Manager/Senior Manager


Lead and manage analytics projects, ensuring timely delivery of high-quality insights to clients

Collaborate with clients to understand business needs and develop tailored analytics solutions

Analyse large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies that inform strategic decision-making

Present findings and recommendations to stakeholders, translating complex data into actionable insights

Support senior leadership with business development activities, including contributing to proposals, pitch materials, and client presentations

Mentor and develop junior team members, fostering a culture of continuous learning and improvement

Stay up to date with industry trends and emerging technologies to enhance service offerings



Requirements

Relevant tertiary qualifications in accounting, business, finance or economics, with a professional accreditation (or international equivalent) preferred

Strong commercial acumen and strategic mindset, with experience across areas such as M&A, strategy, management consulting, corporate finance or accounting

Advanced quantitative and analytical capability, with experience working with large datasets and translating business questions into robust analysis

Advanced proficiency in a leading data analytics platform is essential, as it is the primary tool used to deliver core analytics outputs

Experience with SQL, Python and/or R is highly advantageous, supporting bespoke analysis, coaching of team members, and effective review and troubleshooting

Excellent communication and leadership skills, with the ability to clearly articulate insights through a commercial lens, influence senior stakeholders, and lead teams and projects effectively


Maven Partners

Maven Partners is a specialist financial advisory, accounting and taxation recruitment services business. The partners have a wealth of experience in executive search and interim management, servicing clients across Asia Pacific, from our offices in Melbourne, Sydney and Brisbane.

At Maven Partners, we are holders and seekers of knowledge. In our case this refers to knowledge gained through experience, dedication, and perseverance. Knowledge is Maven's central value.

About Lactalis-Mainland Dairy


Mainland Dairy, the business formerly known as Fonterra’s global Consumer and associated businesses, is now part of the Lactalis Group and known as Lactalis-Mainland Dairy. 
Lactalis is a French family-owned business founded in Laval in 1933. Today, it is the world’s leading dairy group, present in 50 countries employing around 85,500 people. It is home to some of the world’s best known global and international dairy brands, including Président, Galbani and Parmalat, and local brands including Pauls, OAK, and Cracker Barrel in Australia.  


Lactalis-Mainland Dairy is a team of more than 4,300 employees, operating 16 manufacturing sites across Oceania, Southeast Asia, South Asia and the Middle East. We make well-loved brands including Perfect Italiano, Mainland, and Western Star. 


Our passionate and talented team is committed to nurturing the future, unlocking new opportunities, and fostering innovation in nutrition and food.   


The Opportunity


Reporting to the Head of Financial Accounting, this 12-month maximum term contract provides critical FP&A capability during a high intensity period of change and transformation. 


As FP&A Lead, you will play a key role in delivering budgeting, forecasting, reporting, and financial insight activities while supporting the establishment of new operating and reporting models. You will operate across a complex, multinational environment, ensuring accurate, timely and decision useful financial performance insights.


In this role, you will:


Lead delivery of the F26 Budget process, including coordination, consolidation, and performance analysis
Produce monthly reporting, including tailored performance views across sales volumes, brands, categories, and channels
Deliver Head Office forecasting and support Group level forecast consolidation
Support month-end close activities within CFIN, identifying and resolving financial and system issues
Investigate and resolve reporting and consolidation discrepancies across markets and entities
Partner with business stakeholders to support financial design and accounting treatment of new initiatives
Design and operationalise cost allocation methodologies, including TSA, standalone, and head office cost models
Drive improvement in reporting quality, cost centre discipline, and financial data integrity
Respond to ad hoc analysis and insights requests from senior leadership and Group Finance


About You


You are a commercially astute FP&A professional with strong analytical capability and a hands-on approach to delivery. You thrive in complex, fast-changing environments and bring a pragmatic mindset to solving financial and operational challenges.


To succeed in this role, you’ll ideally bring:


8–10 years’ experience in FP&A, commercial finance or group reporting
Proven delivery of annual budget cycles and rolling forecasts in complex organisations
Experience in multinational environments with exposure to group reporting and consolidation processes
Strong understanding of cost allocation frameworks, including TSA and head office structures
Experience resolving financial, systems, and data issues across multiple entities or markets
Hands-on expertise with ERP and reporting systems (e.g. CFIN or similar)
Strong analytical skills, with the ability to generate insights across product, channel and market performance
Demonstrated stakeholder management and business partnering capability
Ability to manage ambiguity, competing priorities and senior stakeholder expectations


What We Offer


Competitive pay and benefits
Career development and internal opportunities across a global business
A team focused workplace in a fantastic culture where your work has real impact
A strong commitment to quality, innovation and sustainability
Flexible work arrangements where possible


Based at our Richmond office, this role offers excellent career development and internal mobility pathways as well as flexible ways of working and a hybrid structure, that allows you the balance of in-office collaboration and working from home to suit your lifestyle. 


Please note that applicants must have full working rights for Australia to be considered.
We are politely declining recruitment agency support and approaches for this role.

About the Role

RPMGlobal (RPM) is seeking a Technical Analyst to join our Mine Planning software development team. This is an opportunity to apply your technical skills in designing software and supporting product users as part of an established development team.

This is a full-time role based in head office in Brisbane CBD. The position is in-office, however, it does allow for ad hoc working from home.

RPM is the market leader in enterprise solutions from mine planning to execution. This role will primarily work on RPM's advanced mine planning software, which is used by mining professionals worldwide to design, evaluate, and optimise mining strategies. These products combine geological data, engineering logic, and powerful algorithms to help clients make informed decisions across diverse mining environments.




Key Responsibilities

Assist with the development of ideas, requests and suggestions of product changes.

Communicate with both the development team and stakeholders in order to implement solutions.

Provide product support to internal users and external customers.

Test software products to provide feedback from a technical point of view.

Analyse, collate and report on test outcomes and contribute to the resolution of any issues.

Develop written communication of technical information and product overviews.




About You

Tertiary qualifications in engineering, information technology or mathematics.

An interest in the mining industry.

Exposure to developing software.

Strong technical mindset with good solution and problem-solving abilities.

Team player attitude with a collaborative approach.

Strong command of the English language in both verbal and written form.

Legally entitled to work in Australia.




Highly Regarded Experience

Exposure to mine planning or geological modelling software such as Maptek Vulcan, Deswik, Datamine, or similar mining packages.

Experience with CAD or spatial design tools (e.g., AutoCAD, MicroStation, GIS platforms).

Understanding of block models, mine design concepts, or geological data structures.

Familiarity with scripting or automation within technical software environments.

Experience working with complex technical products or enterprise software.




About RPM

RPMGlobal is the mining industry’s leading enterprise technology and training provider and has been at the forefront of change and innovation in the mining industry for over 50 years.​​

We advance the mining industry towards a safer, more efficient, and more sustainable future through the delivery of innovative software solutions and deep domain mining expertise. Guided by our underlying principles of mining expertise, innovation, and sustainability, we strive to create safer and more efficient operations for our mining customers.

Our focus on providing advanced mining enterprise technology and training across the mining lifecycle enables us to guide customers through the industry's current and emerging challenges worldwide. This includes helping them meet the shift in social norms and consumer and investor ESG-related expectations.

Program Salesforce Business Analyst
Contract – Melbourne - Onsite
$900 - $1100 a day
 
CalibrX is Australia’s leading Salesforce recruitment agency, and we are partnering with a global Salesforce implementation partner that is looking for a Lead Salesforce Business Analyst to work with their customer in the entertainment industry and take ownership across large-scale, multi-stream transformation programs.
 
This is not a standard BA role. You will be working closely with C-suite stakeholders, program sponsors, architects and delivery teams to shape business requirements, define program roadmaps, manage dependencies, and ensure delivery stays aligned across multiple workstreams.
 
The right person will be comfortable leading from the front, mentoring other BAs, running executive workshops, and bringing structure, clarity and consistency to complex Salesforce programs.
 
Responsibilities:

Lead and mentor a team of Business Analysts across multiple Salesforce program streams
Work closely with C-suite stakeholders, program directors and steering committees
Translate high-level business strategy into clear requirements, roadmaps and delivery plans
Define epics, shape product backlogs and support release planning at a program level
Identify risks, dependencies and impacts across multiple workstreams
Facilitate executive workshops, steering committees and planning sessions
Work closely with Solution Architects and Program Managers to align business outcomes with Salesforce delivery
Experience:

8+ years’ experience in business analysis, ideally within Salesforce consulting or enterprise transformation environments
Strong experience operating as a Lead BA, Program BA or Senior BA across large-scale programs
Experience mentoring or managing other Business Analysts
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, including working with senior executives
Strong understanding of agile delivery, product backlogs, user stories and program-level planning
Experience with tools such as Jira, Azure DevOps, Lucidchart or Visio
Excellent communication skills and the ability to bring structure to complex environments
Why you should apply:

Work on large-scale enterprise Salesforce transformation programs
Work with a global name in entertainment
Work with experienced Salesforce Architects, consultants and delivery teams
Take on a senior role with real influence across program direction and delivery

If you are interested in this role and would like to hear more, hit the apply button with your updated CV and we will contact you.
 
This role is only open to candidates based in Australia with full working rights

Want to bring your consulting skills into engineering and asset management?

At Aurecon, we're an Australian-owned design, engineering and advisory company with a proud history of helping clients manage their assets across the full lifecycle. With strong growth across Australia, we're seeking Consultants to join our national Asset Management Advisory team.

This is an opportunity to move beyond traditional consulting and apply your skills to projects that improve the performance, reliability and sustainability of the infrastructure and assets communities rely on every day.

How you'll make an impact

Our Asset Intelligence Practice is seeking experienced engagement leads with strong digital and consulting skills. This team supports asset decision making and system development for our client base. This role focuses on integrating asset-centric data and decisions with high value digital solutions including:

Asset Strategy (eg, current state assessments, asset management & digital roadmaps)
Asset Information System Design & Implementation (eg, data engineering, BIM design)
Asset Decision Platform Development (eg, data visualisation, advanced analytics)

In this role you will play an integral role in the delivery of our infrastructure-centric advisory services across a range of sectors, including water, defence, resources, transport and energy.

Here Are The Key Things You'll Do:

Leading streams or small projects as well a team member on larger project
Contribute to, or lead, the development of client deliverables, including digital solutions, technical reports, client presentations and advanced analytics
Work on projects that
Contributing to bid development and client engagement
Coaching and mentoring junior team members
Build and maintaining internal and external relationships
Play an active role in supporting the development of Aurecon's national Advisory team and fostering a collaborative and inclusive culture of work.

What you'll bring
To be successful in this position you will ideally have:

Experience in platforms such as Microsoft Power Platform, SQL, Python, low code, cloud
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Well-developed client engagement and project management skills
Experience successfully working in teams
Adaptability & flexibility to work across different engagement types
Clear, concise and effective written and verbal communication skills
A track record of project delivery in a consulting or industry, preferably with experience in asset-intensive industry
A tertiary qualification in STEM, finance or related degree/discipline

We value that each of our team members brings something different to Aurecon. We look for people who have had a broad range of experiences throughout their career and can demonstrate how they have worked as part of a team.

Does that sound like you? APPLY NOW!
